scores.correctness_notes: The response answers a different question — it explains how to *author* a charmlib from scratch (LIBID, LIBAPI, custom events, etc.) rather than how to *use* existing charmlibs to implement a provider relation. The gold standard focuses on: finding the interface in the charmlibs listing, distinguishing PyPI vs Charmhub-hosted libraries, and implementing the provider contract from the interface spec. The patterns described (leader writes to app databag, relation events, Juju Secrets) are technically correct but the overall workflow is wrong.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — LIBID/LIBAPI/LIBPATCH/PYDEPS are real charmlib conventions; ops.Object, ops.RelationEvent, ops.ObjectEvents, ops.EventSource, model_dump() are all legitimate framework APIs
